description: Scans converted QMD files for leaked PMWiki markup and structural issues.

leak_patterns.name: Pipe Table Syntax
leak_patterns.description: PMWiki ||...|| pipe table syntax survived conversion.
leak_patterns.notes: Any line starting with || in a QMD file is a confirmed miss. Attribute rows (||border=1 width=80%||) should have been stripped by the converter.

leak_patterns.name: List Items Without Space
leak_patterns.description: PMWiki list marker without space (*item or #item) passed through unconverted.
leak_patterns.notes: Exclude lines inside fenced code blocks. #%alpha% is not a miss — it should have been converted to <ol type="a">.

leak_patterns.name: Unconverted Headers Inside Blocks
leak_patterns.description: PMWiki !! or !!! header syntax survived inside a toggle or note block.
leak_patterns.notes: May occur if block processing extracted content before header rules fired.

leak_patterns.name: Monospace / Inline Code (@@text@@)
leak_patterns.description: PMWiki @@...@@ inline monospace markup not converted to backtick code.
leak_patterns.notes: Skip occurrences inside fenced code blocks.

leak_patterns.name: Superscript (^text^)
leak_patterns.description: PMWiki ^text^ superscript — Quarto supports this natively but may conflict with adjacent math.
leak_patterns.notes: Flag only if inside a $...$ math region where ^ is LaTeX power syntax. Skip if outside math — Quarto ^text^ is correct output.

leak_patterns.name: Strikethrough ({-text-})
leak_patterns.description: PMWiki {-text-} strikethrough not converted to Markdown ~~text~~.
leak_patterns.notes: No known false positives — this pattern is unambiguous.

leak_patterns.name: Definition Lists (:term:definition)
leak_patterns.description: PMWiki :term:definition list syntax survived in output.
leak_patterns.example_bad: :logarithm:The exponent to which a base must be raised
leak_patterns.example_good:
**logarithm**
: The exponent to which a base must be raised
leak_patterns.notes: Only flag lines that start with : and contain a second : before end-of-line. Avoid matching YAML colons in frontmatter.

leak_patterns.name: Horizontal Rules (----)
leak_patterns.description: PMWiki 4+ dash horizontal rule — also valid Markdown, but verify intent.
leak_patterns.notes: 4+ dashes render correctly in Markdown. Flag as informational only.

leak_patterns.name: Force Line Break (\\)
leak_patterns.description: PMWiki \\ forced line break at end of line — renders as literal \\ in QMD.
leak_patterns.example_bad: See the board\\ for the sketch
leak_patterns.example_good:
See the board  
for the sketch
leak_patterns.notes:
Should be converted to two trailing spaces or
.

leak_patterns.name: Text Size Markup ([+text+], [-text-])
leak_patterns.description: PMWiki [+larger+] or [-smaller-] text sizing passed through as literal brackets.
leak_patterns.notes: Purely cosmetic. No Quarto/Markdown equivalent — strip or convert to bold.

leak_patterns.name: Pipe-Style Links ([[text | url]])
leak_patterns.description: PMWiki [[display | url]] link form using pipe separator instead of ->.
leak_patterns.notes: Converter only handles [[ -> ]] form. Pipe form leaks through.

leak_patterns.name: Compound % Style Markup
leak_patterns.description: Compound PMWiki % markup like %bgcolor=yellow% or %item value=3% survived stripping.
leak_patterns.notes: Basic %word% forms are stripped; compound forms with = may survive. Skip math placeholders (XXMATHXX).

leak_patterns.name: PMWiki Directives ((:...:))
leak_patterns.description: PMWiki server-side directive survived in output — students see raw command text.
leak_patterns.notes: Any (:word match is a confirmed critical issue. No false positives.

leak_patterns.name: Raw Attach: References
leak_patterns.description: Attach:filename not matched by image conversion rule — produces broken link.
leak_patterns.notes: Check for unusual file extensions or Attach: in contexts the regex didn't reach.

leak_patterns.name: URL-Encoded Sequences (%25, %0a, etc.)
leak_patterns.description: URL-encoded sequences survived because URL decoding was partial or failed.
leak_patterns.notes: These appear literally in output. Decode with urllib.parse.unquote.

leak_patterns.name: Raw >>...<<  Div Tags
leak_patterns.description: Surviving >>word<< or >>><< means a div block was not processed.
leak_patterns.notes: Single > is Markdown blockquote — not a miss. Only double >> triggers this check.

leak_patterns.name: Unclosed <details> Tags
leak_patterns.description: Count <details> vs </details> per file — mismatch means a broken toggle block.
leak_patterns.notes: Use count-based check: len(re.findall('<details>', text)) == len(re.findall('</details>', text)). Not a pattern scan — requires file-level count.

critical.description: Broken output students will see — leaked directives, raw syntax, broken links.
critical.action: Block site push. Re-convert or hand-fix before deploying.

high.description: Significantly degraded output — unconverted markup, broken structure.
high.action: Block site push with --strict flag. Fix before next release.

medium.description: Cosmetic or minor semantic issue — rendered output is readable but imperfect.
medium.action: Report but do not block. Fix in next converter update.

low.description: Informational — usually valid output, flagged for awareness.
low.action: Log only. No action required unless patterned.

cross_reference_rules.description: When a leak is found in a QMD file, decode the corresponding wiki flat file to show the original source markup alongside the leak.
steps: Map QMD filename back to wiki page name: site/class/class-N.qmd → Class.N
Read pmwiki_data/wiki.d/Class.N and decode: extract text= field, urllib.parse.unquote()
Find the original line containing the leaked markup (fuzzy match on surrounding words)
Include both the original wiki line and the current QMD line in the report

validation_test_cases.input:
*Take turns acting as scribe
#Complete the exit ticket
Call the function @@draw_pmf@@ with your data.

validation_test_cases.notes: ^ inside math must NOT trigger G5 superscript check.
# and * inside code fences must NOT trigger G2 list checks.

validation_test_cases.input: > This is a Markdown blockquote, not a PMWiki div.
validation_test_cases.notes: Single > must NOT trigger G16 raw div tag check.

preprocessing: Before running any pattern checks, extract fenced code blocks (```...```) and [@...@] blocks into placeholders.
Run all checks against the placeholder-substituted text.
G17 (unclosed details) uses count comparison, not pattern scan — run separately.
implementation_notes.performance_target: Under 10 seconds for all 68 pages on local machine.
